query: What topics are covered in Unit 3 of Data Structures?
why: Unit III of the Data Structures course begins with stacks. This chunk is the only one containing that exact content boundary. A retrieval failure here means students get the wrong unit content.
fail_means: Chunker split Unit III from its parent course block, or the wrong course block was retrieved.

query: How many marks is the internal assessment test worth?
why: The assessment chunk is the only location where internal mark allocations are specified numerically. If this chunk is absent, Vidya cannot answer marks-related questions.
fail_means: Assessment section was merged into a course block chunk and its content is diluted.

why: The prerequisites field exists only in the course block for 2A1COMM01L. It must not be split away from the course title.
fail_means: Course block was split at the prerequisites line, orphaning the prerequisite from its course title.

query: Which textbooks are recommended for Data Structures?
why: Reference books appear only in the Data Structures course block references section. Must not be mixed with Computer Fundamentals references.

why: The five properties are enumerated as a numbered list in section 2.1. The list must not be split mid-enumeration.
